scenario.name: boots_kart_and_floor
scenario.description: kart sprite (OAM slot 0) + multi-color Mode-7 perspective floor render at standstill (B.6 row 4 boot)

scenario.name: accelerate_lights_speed_bar
scenario.description: holding B builds speed: the speed-bar tick lights (dim tile 0x0A -> lit tile 0x08) in OAM (B.6 row 4 hold b)

scenario.name: steer_rotates_floor
scenario.description: after accelerating, steering LEFT vs RIGHT from one shared baseline rotates the Mode-7 floor differently (axis_branch reloads + re-applies the same prelude per branch so palette phase + forward distance match; the only difference between the two endpoint screenshots is the steer direction). Replaces the Phase-1 screenshot_changed form, which read ~78-88% before/after change on BOTH good and frozen-steer ROMs because the floor animates independently — it PASSED on a broken ROM. Audit-1 H-1 remediation drove boot-align + prelude + steer under FRAME-DETERMINISTIC stepping (align_frame absorbs boot jitter) + a phase_guard asserting per-branch heartbeat ($E010) + day-night phase ($E014) MATCH — but audit-2 found a RESIDUAL spurious PASS (1/55, frozen floor diff up to 91.3%) the frame-count guard is blind to: a per-capture render-phase BUCKET assigned independently to each load->capture sequence, identical in every queryable field (R_POSX/Y, R_ANGLE, $E010, $E014, ppu_frame_count) yet differing 6.5-91% in the framebuffer. H-1 ROUND-2 fix (D_stable+retry): each direction also captures a SAME-DIRECTION control; the diff(L,R) reading is trusted ONLY when diff(L,L')<=control_eps AND diff(R,R')<=control_eps (both directions internally bucket-stable); a confounded control RE-DRIVES that direction (fresh load re-rolls the bucket, up to max_retries; fail-loud if exhausted), and the screenshot_axis_diff control_axes gate is the in-assert backstop. Verified over >=100 good + >=100 frozen alternating stress with the control gate active: GOOD floor diff(L,R)=42.4% (rock-stable), CONTROL-GATED frozen diff(L,R)<=0.4% (kart H-flip lean-frame noise; the up-to-91% raw bucket distance is rejected by the control gate before it reaches the diff) — 0 spurious passes, 0 good failures, margin ~42pp at min_frac=0.15. NB the control-gated frozen ceiling (0.4%), NOT the raw bucket distance, is the discrimination figure. (B.6 row 4 steer; audit-1 MED-A; audit-2 H-1; diagnosis docs/audit/oracle_loop_brick5-h1-diagnosis.md)
